front: Photograph: shows a Spanish Loyalist militiaman (Federico Borrell Garcia) at the moment of death — arms flung back, rifle dropping — during the Spanish Civil War
Photograph: D-Day photographs at Omaha Beach; only 11 of over 100 survived after a Life magazine darkroom technician accidentally destroyed the rest
Artist: co-founded Magnum Photos with Henri Cartier-Bresson, David 'Chim' Seymour, and George Rodger
Artist: died stepping on a landmine in French Indochina in 1954
Photograph: authenticity long disputed; Gerda Taro may have taken it; lost negatives found in 'The Mexican Suitcase' fueled the debate; the subject is Federico Borrell Garcia
Artist: collaborated closely with Gerda Taro, who shared his pseudonym ('Robert Capa')
Artist: co-authored A Russian Journal (1948) with John Steinbeck after touring the Soviet Union together
Photograph: Capa waded ashore past 'metal hedgehogs' with the 1st Infantry Division to take these images; his autobiography Slightly Out of Focus is named after their caption
Artist: famous quote: 'If your pictures aren't good enough, you're not close enough'
Photograph: shows a bald woman holding her baby as a crowd marches; depicts a woman accused of collaboration in Chartres, France
back: The Shaved Woman of Chartres (Robert Capa)

front: Photograph: opening sequence of Saving Private Ryan (1998) was visually inspired by this series of D-Day landing images
